articles.title: USD/JPY: Yen slide and BoJ risks – BNY
articles.expand: BNY’s Head of Markets Macro Strategy Bob Savage notes the Japanese Yen continues to weaken with USD/JPY near 160 as JGB yields hit multi‑decade highs and the Bank of Japan (BoJ) maintains a hiking bias.

articles.title: When is the US ISM Services PMI data for March and how could it affect EUR/USD?
articles.expand: The United States (US) Institute of Supply Management (ISM) Services Purchasing Managers’ Index (PMI) data for March is scheduled to be published today at 14:00 GMT.

articles.title: AUD/USD Price Forecast: Jumps to near 0.6930 ahead of Iran’s response to US ceasefire
articles.expand: The AUD/USD pair trades 0.5% higher to near 0.6930 during the European trading session on Monday. The Aussie pair strengthens as riskier assets trade firmly amid ongoing discussions between the United States (US) and Iran regarding an end to the Middle East war.

articles.title: NZD/USD Price Forecast: Kiwi picks up to 0.5720 with bearish pressure easing
articles.expand: The New Zealand Dollar (NZD) is capitalising on a moderate US Dollar (USD) weakness on a calm trading session, with most markets closed on Easter Monday.

articles.title: Pound Sterling bounces back against US Dollar as market sentiment improves
articles.expand: The Pound Sterling attracts significant bids against the US Dollar (USD) on Monday, rising 0.45% to near 1.3255 during the European trading session.

articles.title: USD: Escalation risks support Dollar strength – MUFG
articles.expand: MUFG’s Lloyd Chan notes that persistent geopolitical tensions around Iran are reinforcing USD strength. Elevated US yields, resilient labour data and fading expectations for Fed rate cuts underpin Dollar carry appeal.

articles.title: USD/JPY eases to 159.40 amid hopes of a peace deal in Iran
articles.expand: The US Dollar (USD) nudges lower against the Japanese Yen (JPY) on Monday, with trading volumes at low levels as most markets remain closed on Easter Monday. The pair hit session lows at 159.35 on the early European session, as hopes of a peace deal in Iran are putting the US Dollar under pressure.

articles.title: USD/INR gains as rallying oil prices weigh on Indian Rupee
articles.expand: The Indian Rupee (INR) falls after a flat opening against the US Dollar (USD) at the start of the Reserve Bank of India’s (RBI) monetary policy week.

articles.title: ECB: Recovery outlook and rate path – Societe Generale

articles.title: Australian Dollar holds firm on US-Iran ceasefire optimism
articles.expand: The AUD/USD pair gathers strength around 0.6910 during the early European trading hours on Monday. Hopes for a US-Iran ceasefire provide some support to the riskier asset, such as the Australian Dollar (AUD) against the Greenback.

articles.title: USD/JPY: Overextension near 160 raises BoJ risk – DBS
articles.expand: Philip Wee at DBS Group Research argues that USD/JPY looks overextended as it tests Japan’s 160 pain threshold. The pair is supported by the US-Japan rate differential, but markets now price a roughly two-thirds chance of a Bank of Japan (BoJ) rate hike on April 28.

articles.title: EUR/GBP eases to levels near 0.8700 amid mild risk-off markets
articles.expand: The Euro (EUR) is trading lower against the British Pound (GBP) on a calm week opening, with most markets closed on Easter Monday, and markets wary of risk, amid greater concerns about an escalation of the war in Iran.

articles.title: Euro area inflation rises on energy shock, core trends stay limited
articles.expand: Euro area inflation rose to 2.5% mainly due to higher energy prices driven by tensions in Iran, while core inflation remained subdued. Price pressures have not broadly spread across the economy, but second-round effects may emerge over time. The European Central Bank is likely to respond cautiously, with limited need for aggressive rate hikes.
